narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly shifts the focus of the conflict's cause away from Russia's invasion, instead framing the US as the aggressive party with dreams of defeating Russia, thereby blaming the West for the continuation or nature of the war.

narratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
narratives.reasoning: This statement portrays Western (US/NATO) economic and military support as an external pressure that Russia is bravely standing up to, implicitly criticizing the West's role and framing their support as an obstacle rather than a legitimate response to aggression.

narratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
narratives.reasoning: The phrase 'रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है' (Russia is standing chest-out) glorifies Russia's resilience and defiance against Western pressure, portraying it as strong and unwavering, which is a form of positive commendation of Russia's stance.

narratives.evidence_quote: रूस को अधिकांश नाटो देश अपना विरोधी मानने लगे हैं। रूस के साथ कारोबार के रास्ते अस्थायी रूप से बंद कर चुके हैं।
narratives.reasoning: This statement frames Russia as being unfairly isolated and targeted by NATO countries, who have cut off trade routes. It evokes a sense of Russia being victimized by collective Western opposition.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly shifts responsibility for the conflict's continuation and escalation to the United States, portraying it as the party dreaming of defeating Russia, rather than acknowledging Russia's role as the initial invader.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames Russia's continued aggression as a defensive stance against Western (American/NATO) economic and military support for Ukraine, thereby blaming the West for prolonging the war.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: यूक्रेन की उसके आगे ताकत कहीं कम है, लेकिन विशेष रूप से अमेरिका की मदद और उकसावे से राष्ट्रपति जेलेंस्की भी आक्रामक तेवर अपनाए हुए हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly blames the US for 'provoking' and enabling Ukrainian President Zelenskyy's 'aggressive posture', shifting the narrative of aggression away from Russia and onto Ukraine and its Western backers.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is weak
subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly presents Russia as standing firm and defiant against the combined economic and military support of the US, NATO, and other Western countries, implying that Western pressure and power are insufficient to force Russia to surrender, thus portraying the West as weak and ineffectual.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement frames US support for Ukraine not as genuine concern for Ukraine itself, but as a means to achieve its own strategic goal of defeating Russia, implying the West is using Ukraine for its own interests.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस को अधिकांश नाटो देश अपना विरोधी मानने लगे हैं। रूस के साथ कारोबार के रास्ते अस्थायी रूप से बंद कर चुके हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative of discrediting Western diplomacy by portraying NATO's actions (viewing Russia as an adversary and cutting off trade) as a reactive, divisive, and ultimately isolating stance that contributes to conflict rather than resolution. It doesn't neatly fit the more specific subnarratives but clearly aims to discredit Western diplomatic and economic approaches.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia has international support from a number of countries and people
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस और यूक्रेन युद्ध के बीच रूस और चीन की निकटताओं में एक उभार देखने को मिला है। चीन के राष्ट्रपति शी जिनपिंग ने राष्ट्रपति पुतिन को अपने यहां बुलाकर खूब स्वागत—सत्कार किया। पुतिन ने भी शी को मास्को बुलाया था।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly highlights the strengthening relationship and mutual hospitality between Russia and China, portraying Russia as having significant international support from a major global power, which aligns with the subnarrative of Russia having international support.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia is a guarantor of peace and prosperity
subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement portrays Russia as resolute and steadfast against Western pressure, refusing to surrender. This frames Russia's stance as one of strength and determination, indirectly suggesting it is defending its interests and those of its allies, which is a component of portraying it as a guarantor of a certain order (prosperity for its side).

subnarratives.evidence_quote: उत्तर कोरिया का तानाशाह हर उस काम को करने में दिलचस्पी लेता है जो खासकर अमेरिका को चुभता हो। शायद इसीलिए उसने रूस के पाले में खड़े होकर यूक्रेन पर गोले दागने की राह चुनी है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative by portraying Russia's position in the Ukraine conflict as the correct or attractive one for other nations (like North Korea) that oppose the US. It praises Russia by presenting it as the natural ally for those resisting American influence, which is a positive portrayal of Russia's geopolitical role but does not fit neatly into the other specific subnarrative definitions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: The West is russophobic
subnarratives.evidence_quote: रूस को अधिकांश नाटो देश अपना विरोधी मानने लगे हैं। रूस के साथ कारोबार के रास्ते अस्थायी रूप से बंद कर चुके हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames NATO countries' opposition and sanctions against Russia as a hostile, collective action taken against Russia, implying a prejudiced or antagonistic stance ('विरोधी मानने लगे हैं') rather than a response to Russia's own actions in Ukraine.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Russia actions in Ukraine are only self-defence
subnarratives.evidence_quote: लेकिन अमेरिका, ‘नाटो’ और अन्य पश्चिमी देशों के आर्थिक—सैन्य सहयोग के सामने रूस सीना ताने खड़ा है और हथियार डालने को तैयार नहीं है। यूक्रेन की उसके आगे ताकत कहीं कम है, लेकिन विशेष रूप से अमेरिका की मदद और उकसावे से राष्ट्रपति जेलेंस्की भी आक्रामक तेवर अपनाए हुए हैं।
subnarratives.reasoning: This passage portrays Russia as standing firm ('सीना ताने खड़ा है') against a Western-backed Ukraine, suggesting Russia's continued fight is a defensive stance against Ukrainian aggression fueled by American 'उकसावे' (provocation/incitement), thereby justifying Russia's actions as a necessary response.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: सब जानते हैं, अमेरिका यूक्रेन के पाले में खड़ा है और रूस को परास्त करने के सपने दे रहा है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the 'Russia is the Victim' narrative by depicting the US as actively dreaming of defeating Russia, framing the conflict as one where Russia is the target of American ambitions, which is a distinct argument not fully captured by the other specific subnarratives.
